orchestrator.description: Primary orchestrator — plans, decomposes, and delegates tasks to specialized subagents.

coder.description: Implements code changes following project conventions and TDD practices.

tester.description: Writes and runs tests, reports coverage and quality.

researcher.description: Explores codebase, documentation, and web to gather context.

reviewer.description: Reviews code for quality, style, and potential issues.

debugger.description: Systematic debugging agent for diagnosing issues.

verifier.description: Validates that subagents completed their tasks correctly.

vision.description: Processes images, screenshots, diagrams, and visual content.

context-builder.description: Handles large documents and files that exceed normal context limits.

code-runner.description: Executes code, runs scripts, and manages code execution tasks.

osf:grill.prompt: Ask clarifying questions to understand what the user wants to build. One question at a time. After understanding, suggest creating a plan with osf:plan.

osf:plan.prompt: Create a detailed implementation plan. Break work into independent subtasks. For each subtask, specify which subagent should handle it.

osf:execute.prompt: Ask the user which execution mode they want (auto, review-plan, step-by-step, dry-run, direct), then execute the plan accordingly.

osf:review.prompt: Review the current git diff for bugs, style issues, and improvements. Be specific about file and line numbers.

osf:test.prompt: Run the project's tests and report results. If tests fail, suggest debugging with osf:debug.

osf:debug.prompt: Use the osf-debug skill to systematically diagnose the issue.

osf:deploy.prompt: Ask the user where they want to deploy (platform, project, environment) and how. Never deploy without explicit confirmation.

osf:spec.prompt: Generate a specification document from the user's requirements. Include interfaces, contracts, and acceptance criteria.

osf:ship.description: Full flow: test → review → merge → deploy
osf:ship.prompt: Run the full flow: test, review, then ask user about merge/deploy. Never deploy without explicit confirmation.

osf:finish.prompt: All tasks complete. Present options: merge to main, create PR, keep branch, or discard. Let user choose.

osf:refresh-registry.prompt: Scan for skills in all known locations and regenerate the skill registry.
